meta.description: Core data quality expectations for the NYC Taxi staging table. Covers nullability, value ranges, uniqueness proportions, and row volume. Failures trigger Claude AI diagnosis.

meta.notes: January 2024 should have ~2.9M trips. Floor of 1000 catches a catastrophic load failure or accidental truncation.
Pickup timestamp is the primary time dimension. Any null here breaks all time-based aggregations.
Required for trip duration calculation and mart aggregation.
Allows 1% tolerance for edge cases (e.g. comped rides). Higher null rate signals upstream meter system issue.
Allows 1% tolerance. Null distance with a valid fare is a known GPS outage pattern.
Driver entry — higher null tolerance (5%) is acceptable.
Max 500 miles covers rare long-haul edge cases (e.g. airport transfer). Values above this are likely meter malfunctions.
NYC taxi fare cap is $999 in practice for long-distance. Negative fares are refunds/corrections and should be rare.
NYC TLC legal maximum is 6 passengers. Values of 0 or >6 indicate data entry errors.
Total includes tolls and surcharges. Max 2000 covers extreme airport + toll scenarios.
Timestamps should be highly unique. A proportion near 0 means all rows share the same timestamp — a classic bulk-insert bug or a stuck ETL timestamp.
NYC TLC has 263 official zones (IDs 1–265 with gaps). Out-of-range values indicate a mapping error in the upstream dispatch system.
Same zone ID constraint as pickup_location_id.
